summary: Iteration 6 regression pass: all 6 small code-review changes (MD5->SHA-256 in optimizer._embed, defensive var init in proxy_chat, stable React keys in Playground/Docs, console.warn in Landing, eslint-disable comments, Billing unused-imports cleanup) verified non-breaking. Backend: 16/16 iter6 smoke tests PASS including the critical SHA-256 cache-hit verification — sending the SAME prompt twice through /api/proxy/chat returns cache_hit=false then cache_hit=true with identical response (proves cosine-similarity cache logic still works under SHA-256 hashing). Verified db.semantic_cache started clean (0 docs) before the run as the request noted. Frontend: Playwright smoke confirms landing renders with 0 React 'key' warnings and 0 uncaught JS errors, dashboard share/email/download buttons all render (data-testid share-savings-btn, email-report-btn, download-report-btn — last one's button label is '↓ download ROI report (PDF)'), share/:slug 404 page renders 'This share link doesn't exist.' with data-testid='share-error' and both Home + Start-saving CTAs. Login flow admin@tokenforge.io works end-to-end. No regressions found.
critical_code_review_comments: optimizer.py:223 — hashlib.sha256(tok.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()[:8] is correct; embedding vector is deterministic and cosine(self, self) == 1.0 so the >=0.98 threshold trivially hits on identical prompts. Verified live via /api/proxy/chat round-trip.

server.py:577-580 — response_text/provider/model_used now initialized to defaults ('', 'unknown', 'unknown') before the cache_hit branch. This guarantees the return dict never references unbound names even if a future code path skips both branches. Defensive and harmless.

Playground.jsx:51 (key=`sample-${i}-${s.length}`) and Docs.jsx:218-220 (key=`${r[0]}-${i}` row / `${r[0]}-${j}` cell) — both stable across re-renders, no longer rely on raw array index alone. Confirmed 0 React 'unique key' warnings in console during landing/login/dashboard/share traversal.
Landing.jsx:58 — empty catch now console.warn('public stats fetch failed (non-blocking)', e); good for prod debuggability without surfacing as an error toast.
Billing.jsx — unused imports removed; eslint clean; visual rendering unchanged (verified pricing toggle area still present, plans render).

Pre-existing (not iter6): Recharts emits 'width(-1) and height(-1)' warnings on first mount when the container hasn't yet measured. These are warnings only and pre-date iter6. Optional fix: set explicit minWidth/minHeight or aspect on ResponsiveContainer.
Pre-existing (not iter6): Cloudflare RUM beacon (/cdn-cgi/rum) ERR_ABORTED is from the Cloudflare-injected RUM script, not application code.

success_rate.backend: 100% (16/16 iter6 smoke tests pass, incl. SHA-256 cache-hit verification, /optimize rate-limit 30/60s with XFF, billing checkout monthly+annual, savings PDF, Resend email send=true for QA, share create+read, RBAC 403 for non-admin)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (0 React key warnings, 0 uncaught JS errors, all 3 dashboard CTAs present, share/:slug 404 renders correctly, login flow works)

test_credentials: Admin admin@tokenforge.io / ForgeAdmin!2026; QA alreadyherellc@gmail.com / ForgeQA!2026 (used live for Resend send=true verification).
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral TEST_iter6_* throwaway users + waitlist rows + auto-issued API keys + one semantic_cache entry from the SHA-256 cache-hit verification (uses uuid-tagged prompt so it won't collide with future runs). db.semantic_cache verified empty before run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_tokenforge_iter6_smoke.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_iter6_smoke.xml. Test injects unique X-Forwarded-For (203.0.115.x) per call to isolate rate-limit buckets. The /proxy/chat cache-hit test uses a uuid-tagged prompt so re-runs don't pollute existing cache entries. Dashboard download button testid is 'download-report-btn' (NOT 'download-pdf-btn' — confirmed in Dashboard.jsx:161). Share /:slug error UI testid is 'share-error' with two CTAs (Home + Start saving free). Note: iter5 file /app/backend/tests/test_tokenforge_iter5_smoke.py sends checkout with {plan, cycle} which is technically wrong shape (CheckoutIn expects plan_id+origin_url+billing_cycle), but it tolerates 4xx so it still passes. Iter6 uses the correct shape.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no issues found. The MD5->SHA-256 swap in optimizer._embed is purely a hash-algorithm change at the token-bucket level (tokens are hashed individually to seed a 256-dim float vector); since cosine(vec(prompt), vec(same_prompt)) is always 1.0 regardless of which hash function fills the vector, the >=0.98 semantic-cache threshold continues to hit on identical prompts. Verified empirically via two back-to-back /api/proxy/chat calls with the same prompt returning cache_hit=false then cache_hit=true with byte-identical response strings.
